statement: A uniform slender rod of mass 2 kg and length 0.8 m is pinned at its center G and held horizontal. It is released from rest. Find the angular acceleration immediately after release. The moment of inertia of a slender rod about its center is I_G = (1/12)·m·L². Use g = 9.81 m/s².
common_student_errors: When the rod is released from horizontal, gravity on both halves creates equal and opposite torques, resulting in zero net torque — students often miss this and compute a non-zero α
Using I = (1/3)·m·L² (about end) instead of I_G = (1/12)·m·L² (about center)
Computing g·(L/2) as the moment arm without realizing the two halves cancel
